title: Personal Protective Equipment (PPE)
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of Personal Protective Equipment (PPE).
Identify different types of PPE used in industries.
Learn when and how PPE should be used.

introduction.title: What is PPE?
introduction.content: Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) consists of protective clothing and equipment designed to minimize exposure to workplace hazards. PPE acts as the last line of defense when engineering controls and administrative controls cannot completely eliminate risks.

business_purpose.title: Why is PPE Important?
business_purpose.content: PPE protects employees from injuries caused by mechanical, chemical, electrical, thermal and environmental hazards. Proper use of PPE significantly reduces workplace accidents and supports a strong safety culture.

ppe_categories.purpose: Protects the head from falling or moving objects.
Protect the eyes from dust, sparks, chemicals and flying particles.
Provides additional protection against chemical splashes and hot metal.
Prevents inhalation of harmful dust, fumes and gases.
Protect hands from cuts, chemicals, burns and abrasions.
Protects the body from heat, chemicals and sharp objects.
Protect feet from heavy objects, slips and punctures.
Prevents falls while working at heights.

selection_guidelines: Choose PPE appropriate for the task.
maintenance_guidelines: Store in a dry and clean location.
Avoid exposure to direct sunlight if not recommended.
limitations: Regular inspection and replacement are essential.
daily_workflow: Use PPE throughout the assigned task.

integration.purpose: Mandatory PPE protects operators during manufacturing.
Technicians use specialized PPE during repair activities.
Inspectors wear PPE while performing inspections.
Warehouse personnel wear PPE while handling materials.
Emergency teams require specialized protective equipment.

business_example.title: PPE at JSL
business_example.content: Before entering the production area, employees wear safety helmets, safety shoes, protective clothing and eye protection. Personnel working near furnaces additionally wear heat-resistant gloves, face shields and protective clothing designed for high-temperature environments.

common_errors: Removing PPE while still in hazardous areas.
important_notes: PPE is the last line of defense against workplace hazards.
Engineering controls should always be implemented before relying solely on PPE.
Every employee is responsible for maintaining their assigned PPE.
Failure to wear PPE may result in serious injuries.
